purpose: Top-level build definition. Adds the vendored JUCE under lib/juce, defines the 'Light Host' GUI app target via juce_add_gui_app, links the required JUCE modules, compiles the four Source/*.cpp translation units, generates BinaryData from Resources/MicrophoneIcon.png, and applies Windows + MSVC-specific flags.
invariants: cmake_minimum_required is 4.2 — required by the Visual Studio 18 2026 generator. Raising the floor is fine; lowering it breaks the VS2026 build.
CMAKE_SYSTEM_VERSION must be set BEFORE project() so the VS generator picks up WindowsTargetPlatformVersion correctly. Pinned to Win11 26100.
LANGUAGES C CXX is required because JUCE pulls in C translation units (notably juce_graphics_Sheenbidi.c) on Linux. Removing C makes Linux configure fail at CMAKE_C_COMPILE_OBJECT.
C++ standard is fixed to C++23, REQUIRED ON, EXTENSIONS OFF.
Project VERSION (currently 4.0.2) is the single source of truth — propagated to JUCE_APPLICATION_VERSION_STRING via generator expression and to the GitHub Release zip filename via CI.
JUCE_PLUGINHOST_VST3 / VST / AU = 1 are required for the host to load plugins; HostStartup.cpp has a #error guarding this.
JUCE_DIR override (-DJUCE_DIR=<path>) is supported but the default points at the vendored lib/juce.
The 'Light Host' display name comes from juce_add_gui_app's PRODUCT_NAME — the CMake target itself is named LightHost; release artefact paths key off PRODUCT_NAME (e.g. 'Light Host.exe').
gotchas: DelayProcessor.hpp appears in target_sources even though it's header-only — IDEs (VS2026 in particular) need this to show the file in the solution. Removing it does NOT break the build but hides the file in the IDE tree.
JUCE 8.0.13+ moved to createEditorAndMakeActive() — this CMakeLists is fine, but PluginWindow.cpp has a version-specific note that depends on the JUCE version pinned in lib/juce.
ASIO=1 is enabled but lib/ does not vendor the ASIO SDK — Windows builds rely on JUCE's stub headers; full ASIO support requires Steinberg's separately-licensed SDK at build time.
/wd5105 suppresses a warning from JUCE's bundled HarfBuzz; do not enable C5105 elsewhere or unrelated warnings will be hidden too.
